== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4490005000975 == Commons category (P373) == * Korizu-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県高知市鴨部上町5番8号', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Korizu-jinja}} [[JA:郡頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q20044233}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Korizu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Korizu-jinja haiden-2.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}5-8 Kamobe Kamimachi |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|6.80|N|133|29|56.93|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Korizu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 22|Q3025|WD=Q3025|1=November 22}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Korizu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Korizu-jinja torii-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Korizu Shrine''' (こおりずじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamobe Kamimachi, {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} === History === [[File:Korizu-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] === Founding === The founding date is unknown{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}' entry for {{ill|lt=Jingo-keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|WD=Q29961|1=768}}), 11th month, 18th day, it records that 41 people including Kamiyoda no Sukuna from Tosa District, Tosa Province, were granted the surname "{{ill|lt=Kamo no Ason clan|Q122979457|WD=Q122979457|lt=Kamo|1=Kamo no Ason clan}}"; "Kamiyoda" is said to refer to the nearby place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Also, in '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}' under Settsu Province, divine lineage, Chigi, Kamo-be-hafuri entry, it says: "Kamo-be-hafuri, same ancestor as Kamo no Ason, descendants of Ōkuninushi"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. From these records, it is said this shrine began when Kamo/Kamobe groups (be-min of the Kamo clan) living around Kamobe enshrined Ōkuninushi as ancestral deity{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（平凡社）|1983}}. This area is identified with Kamobe-gō of Tosa District in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}'{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Development of Kamobe-gō is seen as from the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}, and primitive rituals are inferred by around the {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090|1=6th century}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later prosperity is seen in a Tōdaiji document: in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=752|Q23639|WD=Q23639|1=752}}), 50 households from Kamobe-gō were assigned as {{ill|lt=fuko|Q11458391|WD=Q11458391|1=Fūko}} of {{ill|lt=Tōdai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The shrine name "Korigashira/Koorizu" has unclear origin: theories include that it stands in Tosa District, the head district of Tosa Province; that district headquarters stood in Kamobe-gō; or that it derives from the place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. === Overview history === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Korizu Shrine" is listed in Tosa District, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, identified with this shrine{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The reading there is given as "Kohoritsuno"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1504|Q6148|WD=Q6148|1=1504}}) and {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1708|Q6846|WD=Q6846|1=1708}}) suggest that from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward the common shrine name became "Kamobe Shrine"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 56 ({{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}}), a fire destroyed the shrine buildings; the current buildings are rebuilt afterward. === Sessha / Massha === [[File:Korizu-jinja keidaisha-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Precinct shrine}}]] * Kōjin-sha / Hachimangū merged shrine === Annual rites === * Saitan-sai (Jan 1)Rite list from precinct information board. * Kinen-sai (lunar Jan 22) * Great Purification / Nagoshi-sai (Jun 30) * Summer festival (Jul 21) * Grand annual festival (Nov 22) * Great Purification / New Year's Eve festival (Dec 31) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Korizu == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4490005000975 == Commons category (P373) == * Korizu-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県高知市鴨部上町5番8号', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Korizu-jinja}} [[JA:郡頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q20044233}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Korizu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Korizu-jinja haiden-2.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}5-8 Kamobe Kamimachi |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|6.80|N|133|29|56.93|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Korizu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 22|Q3025|WD=Q3025|1=November 22}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Korizu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Korizu-jinja torii-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Korizu Shrine''' (こおりずじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamobe Kamimachi, {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} === History === [[File:Korizu-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] === Founding === The founding date is unknown{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}' entry for {{ill|lt=Jingo-keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|WD=Q29961|1=768}}), 11th month, 18th day, it records that 41 people including Kamiyoda no Sukuna from Tosa District, Tosa Province, were granted the surname "{{ill|lt=Kamo no Ason clan|Q122979457|WD=Q122979457|lt=Kamo|1=Kamo no Ason clan}}"; "Kamiyoda" is said to refer to the nearby place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Also, in '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}' under Settsu Province, divine lineage, Chigi, Kamo-be-hafuri entry, it says: "Kamo-be-hafuri, same ancestor as Kamo no Ason, descendants of Ōkuninushi"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. From these records, it is said this shrine began when Kamo/Kamobe groups (be-min of the Kamo clan) living around Kamobe enshrined Ōkuninushi as ancestral deity{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（平凡社）|1983}}. This area is identified with Kamobe-gō of Tosa District in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}'{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Development of Kamobe-gō is seen as from the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}, and primitive rituals are inferred by around the {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090|1=6th century}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later prosperity is seen in a Tōdaiji document: in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=752|Q23639|WD=Q23639|1=752}}), 50 households from Kamobe-gō were assigned as {{ill|lt=fuko|Q11458391|WD=Q11458391|1=Fūko}} of {{ill|lt=Tōdai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The shrine name "Korigashira/Koorizu" has unclear origin: theories include that it stands in Tosa District, the head district of Tosa Province; that district headquarters stood in Kamobe-gō; or that it derives from the place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. === Overview history === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Korizu Shrine" is listed in Tosa District, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, identified with this shrine{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The reading there is given as "Kohoritsuno"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1504|Q6148|WD=Q6148|1=1504}}) and {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1708|Q6846|WD=Q6846|1=1708}}) suggest that from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward the common shrine name became "Kamobe Shrine"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 56 ({{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}}), a fire destroyed the shrine buildings; the current buildings are rebuilt afterward. === Sessha / Massha === [[File:Korizu-jinja keidaisha-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Precinct shrine}}]] * Kōjin-sha / Hachimangū merged shrine === Annual rites === * Saitan-sai (Jan 1)Rite list from precinct information board. * Kinen-sai (lunar Jan 22) * Great Purification / Nagoshi-sai (Jun 30) * Summer festival (Jul 21) * Grand annual festival (Nov 22) * Great Purification / New Year's Eve festival (Dec 31) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Korizu == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4490005000975 == Commons category (P373) == * Korizu-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県高知市鴨部上町5番8号', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Korizu-jinja}} [[JA:郡頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q20044233}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Korizu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Korizu-jinja haiden-2.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}5-8 Kamobe Kamimachi |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|6.80|N|133|29|56.93|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Korizu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 22|Q3025|WD=Q3025|1=November 22}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Korizu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Korizu-jinja torii-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Korizu Shrine''' (こおりずじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamobe Kamimachi, {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} === History === [[File:Korizu-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] === Founding === The founding date is unknown{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}' entry for {{ill|lt=Jingo-keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|WD=Q29961|1=768}}), 11th month, 18th day, it records that 41 people including Kamiyoda no Sukuna from Tosa District, Tosa Province, were granted the surname "{{ill|lt=Kamo no Ason clan|Q122979457|WD=Q122979457|lt=Kamo|1=Kamo no Ason clan}}"; "Kamiyoda" is said to refer to the nearby place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Also, in '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}' under Settsu Province, divine lineage, Chigi, Kamo-be-hafuri entry, it says: "Kamo-be-hafuri, same ancestor as Kamo no Ason, descendants of Ōkuninushi"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. From these records, it is said this shrine began when Kamo/Kamobe groups (be-min of the Kamo clan) living around Kamobe enshrined Ōkuninushi as ancestral deity{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（平凡社）|1983}}. This area is identified with Kamobe-gō of Tosa District in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}'{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Development of Kamobe-gō is seen as from the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}, and primitive rituals are inferred by around the {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090|1=6th century}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later prosperity is seen in a Tōdaiji document: in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=752|Q23639|WD=Q23639|1=752}}), 50 households from Kamobe-gō were assigned as {{ill|lt=fuko|Q11458391|WD=Q11458391|1=Fūko}} of {{ill|lt=Tōdai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The shrine name "Korigashira/Koorizu" has unclear origin: theories include that it stands in Tosa District, the head district of Tosa Province; that district headquarters stood in Kamobe-gō; or that it derives from the place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. === Overview history === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Korizu Shrine" is listed in Tosa District, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, identified with this shrine{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The reading there is given as "Kohoritsuno"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1504|Q6148|WD=Q6148|1=1504}}) and {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1708|Q6846|WD=Q6846|1=1708}}) suggest that from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward the common shrine name became "Kamobe Shrine"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 56 ({{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}}), a fire destroyed the shrine buildings; the current buildings are rebuilt afterward. === Sessha / Massha === [[File:Korizu-jinja keidaisha-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Precinct shrine}}]] * Kōjin-sha / Hachimangū merged shrine === Annual rites === * Saitan-sai (Jan 1)Rite list from precinct information board. * Kinen-sai (lunar Jan 22) * Great Purification / Nagoshi-sai (Jun 30) * Summer festival (Jul 21) * Grand annual festival (Nov 22) * Great Purification / New Year's Eve festival (Dec 31) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Korizu Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/432}} 'Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gōsha Ge'] (NDL Digital Collection), frame 432. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Korizu Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|郡頭神社（平凡社）|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai|author=Yamamoto Dai|authorlink=山本大|chapter=Korizu Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} category|Category:Korizu-jinja}} [[JA:郡頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q20044233}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Korizu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Korizu-jinja haiden-2.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}5-8 Kamobe Kamimachi |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|6.80|N|133|29|56.93|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Korizu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 22|Q3025|WD=Q3025|1=November 22}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Korizu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Korizu-jinja torii-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Korizu Shrine''' (こおりずじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamobe Kamimachi, {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} === History === [[File:Korizu-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] === Founding === The founding date is unknown{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}' entry for {{ill|lt=Jingo-keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|WD=Q29961|1=768}}), 11th month, 18th day, it records that 41 people including Kamiyoda no Sukuna from Tosa District, Tosa Province, were granted the surname "{{ill|lt=Kamo no Ason clan|Q122979457|WD=Q122979457|lt=Kamo|1=Kamo no Ason clan}}"; "Kamiyoda" is said to refer to the nearby place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Also, in '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}' under Settsu Province, divine lineage, Chigi, Kamo-be-hafuri entry, it says: "Kamo-be-hafuri, same ancestor as Kamo no Ason, descendants of Ōkuninushi"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. From these records, it is said this shrine began when Kamo/Kamobe groups (be-min of the Kamo clan) living around Kamobe enshrined Ōkuninushi as ancestral deity{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（平凡社）|1983}}. This area is identified with Kamobe-gō of Tosa District in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}'{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Development of Kamobe-gō is seen as from the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}, and primitive rituals are inferred by around the {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090|1=6th century}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later prosperity is seen in a Tōdaiji document: in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=752|Q23639|WD=Q23639|1=752}}), 50 households from Kamobe-gō were assigned as {{ill|lt=fuko|Q11458391|WD=Q11458391|1=Fūko}} of {{ill|lt=Tōdai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The shrine name "Korigashira/Koorizu" has unclear origin: theories include that it stands in Tosa District, the head district of Tosa Province; that district headquarters stood in Kamobe-gō; or that it derives from the place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. === Overview history === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Korizu Shrine" is listed in Tosa District, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, identified with this shrine{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The reading there is given as "Kohoritsuno"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1504|Q6148|WD=Q6148|1=1504}}) and {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1708|Q6846|WD=Q6846|1=1708}}) suggest that from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward the common shrine name became "Kamobe Shrine"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 56 ({{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}}), a fire destroyed the shrine buildings; the current buildings are rebuilt afterward. === Sessha / Massha === [[File:Korizu-jinja keidaisha-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Precinct shrine}}]] * Kōjin-sha / Hachimangū merged shrine === Annual rites === * Saitan-sai (Jan 1)Rite list from precinct == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4490005000975 == Commons category (P373) == * Korizu-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県高知市鴨部上町5番8号', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Korizu-jinja}} [[JA:郡頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q20044233}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Korizu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Korizu-jinja haiden-2.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}5-8 Kamobe Kamimachi |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|6.80|N|133|29|56.93|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Korizu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 22|Q3025|WD=Q3025|1=November 22}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Korizu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Korizu-jinja torii-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Korizu Shrine''' (こおりずじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamobe Kamimachi, {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} === History === [[File:Korizu-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] === Founding === The founding date is unknown{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}' entry for {{ill|lt=Jingo-keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|WD=Q29961|1=768}}), 11th month, 18th day, it records that 41 people including Kamiyoda no Sukuna from Tosa District, Tosa Province, were granted the surname "{{ill|lt=Kamo no Ason clan|Q122979457|WD=Q122979457|lt=Kamo|1=Kamo no Ason clan}}"; "Kamiyoda" is said to refer to the nearby place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Also, in '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}' under Settsu Province, divine lineage, Chigi, Kamo-be-hafuri entry, it says: "Kamo-be-hafuri, same ancestor as Kamo no Ason, descendants of Ōkuninushi"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. From these records, it is said this shrine began when Kamo/Kamobe groups (be-min of the Kamo clan) living around Kamobe enshrined Ōkuninushi as ancestral deity{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（平凡社）|1983}}. This area is identified with Kamobe-gō of Tosa District in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}'{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Development of Kamobe-gō is seen as from the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}, and primitive rituals are inferred by around the {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090|1=6th century}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later prosperity is seen in a Tōdaiji document: in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=752|Q23639|WD=Q23639|1=752}}), 50 households from Kamobe-gō were assigned as {{ill|lt=fuko|Q11458391|WD=Q11458391|1=Fūko}} of {{ill|lt=Tōdai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The shrine name "Korigashira/Koorizu" has unclear origin: theories include that it stands in Tosa District, the head district of Tosa Province; that district headquarters stood in Kamobe-gō; or that it derives from the place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. === Overview history === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Korizu Shrine" is listed in Tosa District, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, identified with this shrine{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The reading there is given as "Kohoritsuno"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1504|Q6148|WD=Q6148|1=1504}}) and {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1708|Q6846|WD=Q6846|1=1708}}) suggest that from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward the common shrine name became "Kamobe Shrine"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 56 ({{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}}), a fire destroyed the shrine buildings; the current buildings are rebuilt afterward. === Sessha / Massha === [[File:Korizu-jinja keidaisha-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Precinct shrine}}]] * Kōjin-sha / Hachimangū merged shrine === Annual rites === * Saitan-sai (Jan 1)Rite list from precinct information board. * Kinen-sai (lunar Jan 22) * Great Purification / Nagoshi-sai (Jun 30) * Summer festival (Jul 21) * Grand annual festival (Nov 22) * Great Purification / New Year's Eve festival == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4490005000975 == Commons category (P373) == * Korizu-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県高知市鴨部上町5番8号', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Korizu-jinja}} [[JA:郡頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q20044233}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Korizu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Korizu-jinja haiden-2.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}5-8 Kamobe Kamimachi |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|6.80|N|133|29|56.93|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Korizu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 22|Q3025|WD=Q3025|1=November 22}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Korizu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Korizu-jinja torii-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Korizu Shrine''' (こおりずじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamobe Kamimachi, {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} === History === [[File:Korizu-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] === Founding === The founding date is unknown{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}' entry for {{ill|lt=Jingo-keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|WD=Q29961|1=768}}), 11th month, 18th day, it records that 41 people including Kamiyoda no Sukuna from Tosa District, Tosa Province, were granted the surname "{{ill|lt=Kamo no Ason clan|Q122979457|WD=Q122979457|lt=Kamo|1=Kamo no Ason clan}}"; "Kamiyoda" is said to refer to the nearby place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Also, in '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}' under Settsu Province, divine lineage, Chigi, Kamo-be-hafuri entry, it says: "Kamo-be-hafuri, same ancestor as Kamo no Ason, descendants of Ōkuninushi"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. From these records, it is said this shrine began when Kamo/Kamobe groups (be-min of the Kamo clan) living around Kamobe enshrined Ōkuninushi as ancestral deity{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（平凡社）|1983}}. This area is identified with Kamobe-gō of Tosa District in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}'{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Development of Kamobe-gō is seen as from the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}, and primitive rituals are inferred by around the {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090|1=6th century}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later prosperity is seen in a Tōdaiji document: in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=752|Q23639|WD=Q23639|1=752}}), 50 households from Kamobe-gō were assigned as {{ill|lt=fuko|Q11458391|WD=Q11458391|1=Fūko}} of {{ill|lt=Tōdai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The shrine name "Korigashira/Koorizu" has unclear origin: theories include that it stands in Tosa District, the head district of Tosa Province; that district headquarters stood in Kamobe-gō; or that it derives from the place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. === Overview history === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Korizu Shrine" is listed in Tosa District, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, identified with this shrine{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The reading there is given as "Kohoritsuno"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1504|Q6148|WD=Q6148|1=1504}}) and {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1708|Q6846|WD=Q6846|1=1708}}) suggest that from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward the common shrine name became "Kamobe Shrine"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 56 ({{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}}), a fire destroyed the shrine buildings; the current buildings are rebuilt afterward. === Sessha / Massha === [[File:Korizu-jinja keidaisha-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Precinct shrine}}]] * Kōjin-sha / Hachimangū merged shrine === Annual rites === * Saitan-sai (Jan 1)Rite list from precinct information board. * Kinen-sai (lunar Jan 22) * Great Purification / Nagoshi-sai (Jun 30) * Summer festival (Jul 21) * Grand annual festival (Nov 22) * Great Purification / New Year's Eve festival Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|qid=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|qid=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}5-8 Kamobe Kamimachi |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|6.80|N|133|29|56.93|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Korizu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 22|Q3025|qid=Q3025|1=November 22}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Korizu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Korizu-jinja torii-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Korizu Shrine''' (こおりずじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamobe Kamimachi, {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|qid=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|qid=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} === History === [[File:Korizu-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] === Founding === The founding date is unknown{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|qid=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}' entry for {{ill|lt=Jingo-keiun|Q1373049|qid=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|qid=Q29961|1=768}}), 11th month, 18th day, it records that 41 people including Kamiyoda no Sukuna from Tosa District, Tosa Province, were granted the surname "{{ill|lt=Kamo no Ason clan|Q122979457|qid=Q122979457|lt=Kamo|1=Kamo no Ason clan}}"; "Kamiyoda" is said to refer to the nearby place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Also, in '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|qid=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}' under Settsu Province, divine lineage, Chigi, Kamo-be-hafuri entry, it says: "Kamo-be-hafuri, same ancestor as Kamo no Ason, descendants of Ōkuninushi"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. From these records, it is said this shrine began when Kamo/Kamobe groups (be-min of the Kamo clan) living around Kamobe enshrined Ōkuninushi as ancestral deity{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（平凡社）|1983}}. This area is identified with Kamobe-gō of Tosa District in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}'{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Development of Kamobe-gō is seen as from the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|qid=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}, and primitive rituals are inferred by around the {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|qid=Q8090|1=6th century}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later prosperity is seen in a Tōdaiji document: in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|qid=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=752|Q23639|qid=Q23639|1=752}}), 50 households from Kamobe-gō were assigned as {{ill|lt=fuko|Q11458391|qid=Q11458391|1=Fūko}} of {{ill|lt=Tōdai-ji|Q460367|qid=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The shrine name "Korigashira/Koorizu" has unclear origin: theories include that it stands in Tosa District, the head district of Tosa Province; that district headquarters stood in Kamobe-gō; or that it derives from the place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. === Overview history === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), "Korizu Shrine" is listed in Tosa District, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|qid=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, identified with this shrine{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The reading there is given as "Kohoritsuno"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|qid=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1504|Q6148|qid=Q6148|1=1504}}) and {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1708|Q6846|qid=Q6846|1=1708}}) suggest that from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward the common shrine name became "Kamobe Shrine"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 56 ({{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|qid=Q2437|1=1981}}), a fire destroyed the shrine buildings; the current buildings are rebuilt afterward. === Sessha / Massha === [[File:Korizu-jinja keidaisha-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Precinct shrine}}]] * Kōjin-sha / Hachimangū merged shrine === Annual rites === * Saitan-sai (Jan 1)Rite list from precinct information board. * Kinen-sai (lunar Jan 22) * Great Purification / Nagoshi-sai (Jun 30) * Summer festival (Jul 21) * Grand annual festival (Nov 22) * Great Purification / New Year's Eve festival (Dec 31) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Korizu Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|qid=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4490005000975 == Commons category (P373) == * Korizu-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県高知市鴨部上町5番8号', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Korizu-jinja}} [[JA:郡頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q20044233}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Korizu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Korizu-jinja haiden-2.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|qid=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|qid=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}5-8 Kamobe Kamimachi |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|6.80|N|133|29|56.93|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Korizu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 22|Q3025|qid=Q3025|1=November 22}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Korizu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Korizu-jinja torii-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Korizu Shrine''' (こおりずじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamobe Kamimachi, {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|qid=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|qid=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} === History === [[File:Korizu-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] === Founding === The founding date is unknown{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|qid=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}' entry for {{ill|lt=Jingo-keiun|Q1373049|qid=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|qid=Q29961|1=768}}), 11th month, 18th day, it records that 41 people including Kamiyoda no Sukuna from Tosa District, Tosa Province, were granted the surname "{{ill|lt=Kamo no Ason clan|Q122979457|qid=Q122979457|lt=Kamo|1=Kamo no Ason clan}}"; "Kamiyoda" is said to refer to the nearby place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Also, in '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|qid=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}' under Settsu Province, divine lineage, Chigi, Kamo-be-hafuri entry, it says: "Kamo-be-hafuri, same ancestor as Kamo no Ason, descendants of Ōkuninushi"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. From these records, it is said this shrine began when Kamo/Kamobe groups (be-min of the Kamo clan) living around Kamobe enshrined Ōkuninushi as ancestral deity{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（平凡社）|1983}}. This area is identified with Kamobe-gō of Tosa District in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}'{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Development of Kamobe-gō is seen as from the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|qid=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}, and primitive rituals are inferred by around the {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|qid=Q8090|1=6th century}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later prosperity is seen in a Tōdaiji document: in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|qid=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=752|Q23639|qid=Q23639|1=752}}), 50 households from Kamobe-gō were assigned as {{ill|lt=fuko|Q11458391|qid=Q11458391|1=Fūko}} of {{ill|lt=Tōdai-ji|Q460367|qid=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The shrine name "Korigashira/Koorizu" has unclear origin: theories include that it stands in Tosa District, the head district of Tosa Province; that district headquarters stood in Kamobe-gō; or that it derives from the place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. === Overview history === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), "Korizu Shrine" is listed in Tosa District, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|qid=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, identified with this shrine{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The reading there is given as "Kohoritsuno"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|qid=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1504|Q6148|qid=Q6148|1=1504}}) and {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1708|Q6846|qid=Q6846|1=1708}}) suggest that from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward the common shrine name became "Kamobe Shrine"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 56 ({{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|qid=Q2437|1=1981}}), a fire destroyed the shrine buildings; the current buildings are rebuilt afterward. === Sessha / Massha === [[File:Korizu-jinja keidaisha-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Precinct shrine}}]] * Kōjin-sha / Hachimangū merged shrine === Annual rites === * Saitan-sai (Jan 1)Rite list from precinct information board. * Kinen-sai (lunar Jan 22) * Great Purification / Nagoshi-sai (Jun 30) * Summer festival (Jul 21) * Grand annual festival (Nov 22) * Great Purification / New Year's Eve festival (Dec 31) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Korizu Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|qid=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|郡頭神社（平凡社）|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai|author=Yamamoto == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4490005000975 == Commons category (P373) == * Korizu-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県高知市鴨部上町5番8号', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Korizu-jinja}} [[JA:郡頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q20044233}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Korizu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Korizu-jinja haiden-2.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|qid=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|qid=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}5-8 Kamobe Kamimachi |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|6.80|N|133|29|56.93|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Korizu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 22|Q3025|qid=Q3025|1=November 22}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Korizu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Korizu-jinja torii-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Korizu Shrine''' (こおりずじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamobe Kamimachi, {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|qid=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|qid=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} === History === [[File:Korizu-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] === Founding === The founding date is unknown{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|qid=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}' entry for {{ill|lt=Jingo-keiun|Q1373049|qid=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|qid=Q29961|1=768}}), 11th month, 18th day, it records that 41 people including Kamiyoda no Sukuna from Tosa District, Tosa Province, were granted the surname "{{ill|lt=Kamo no Ason clan|Q122979457|qid=Q122979457|lt=Kamo|1=Kamo no Ason clan}}"; "Kamiyoda" is said to refer to the nearby place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Also, in '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|qid=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}' under Settsu Province, divine lineage, Chigi, Kamo-be-hafuri entry, it says: "Kamo-be-hafuri, same ancestor as Kamo no Ason, descendants of Ōkuninushi"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. From these records, it is said this shrine began when Kamo/Kamobe groups (be-min of the Kamo clan) living around Kamobe enshrined Ōkuninushi as ancestral deity{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（平凡社）|1983}}. This area is identified with Kamobe-gō of Tosa District in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}'{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Development of Kamobe-gō is seen as from the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|qid=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}, and primitive rituals are inferred by around the {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|qid=Q8090|1=6th century}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later prosperity is seen in a Tōdaiji document: in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|qid=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=752|Q23639|qid=Q23639|1=752}}), 50 households from Kamobe-gō were assigned as {{ill|lt=fuko|Q11458391|qid=Q11458391|1=Fūko}} of {{ill|lt=Tōdai-ji|Q460367|qid=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The shrine name "Korigashira/Koorizu" has unclear origin: theories include that it stands in Tosa District, the head district of Tosa Province; that district headquarters stood in Kamobe-gō; or that it derives from the place name Kōda{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. === Overview history === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), "Korizu Shrine" is listed in Tosa District, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|qid=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, identified with this shrine{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. The reading there is given as "Kohoritsuno"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|qid=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1504|Q6148|qid=Q6148|1=1504}}) and {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1708|Q6846|qid=Q6846|1=1708}}) suggest that from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward the common shrine name became "Kamobe Shrine"{{Sfn|郡頭神社（式内社）|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 56 ({{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|qid=Q2437|1=1981}}), a fire destroyed the shrine buildings; the current buildings are rebuilt afterward. === Sessha / Massha === [[File:Korizu-jinja keidaisha-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Precinct shrine}}]] * Kōjin-sha / Hachimangū merged shrine === Annual rites === * Saitan-sai (Jan 1)Rite list from precinct
